pattern: A self-reported completion signal is not the completion gate — always verify against the independent checker that actually owns the pass/fail decision before trusting it and stopping.
detail: Watch for: An agent's own status report ('all tests pass', 'N/N criteria met', 'no errors found') is treated as sufficient without re-running the actual gate (CI, linter, AC checklist owner, test suite) that produces the authoritative verdict; A summarizing or self-grading step sits between the work and the real verifier, creating room for the summary to drift from what the verifier would actually find; Repeated cycles of 'looks done' followed by a stricter check finding leftover issues, without adjusting trust in the looser check after the pattern repeats; Any process where the same agent (or a lightly-modified prompt of it) both performs the work and certifies its own success, rather than a separate, differently-scoped process doing the certification. Generalized from: Do not trust the AC self-audit's '0 unmet/blocked' result as proof of completion — cross-check against the actual criteria-fix gate.
tags: principle

An agent's own status report ('all tests pass', 'N/N criteria met', 'no errors found') is treated as sufficient without re-running the actual gate (CI, linter, AC checklist owner, test suite) that produces the authoritative verdict
A summarizing or self-grading step sits between the work and the real verifier, creating room for the summary to drift from what the verifier would actually find
Repeated cycles of 'looks done' followed by a stricter check finding leftover issues, without adjusting trust in the looser check after the pattern repeats
Any process where the same agent (or a lightly-modified prompt of it) both performs the work and certifies its own success, rather than a separate, differently-scoped process doing the certification
safety_reason: Requires re-verifying against an independent authoritative checker instead of trusting a self-reported completion signal, which adds oversight rather than reducing it.

pattern: When acceptance criteria will be machine-verified by inspecting a test file's assertions, write assertions in the first pass that concretely and completely check the behavior described in the Given/Wh
detail: Watch for: Component/unit tests scaffolded with rendering or snapshot checks but no explicit assertion tied to the specific behavior the ticket describes; Acceptance criteria phrased as Given/When/Then that map 1:1 to a test file, where an automated gate inspects assertion presence/content rather than just test pass/fail; Tests written quickly to satisfy 'a test exists' without re-reading the acceptance criteria to confirm every clause (each 'Then') has a corresponding assertion; Any implementation submitted without a self-check pass comparing test assertions line-by-line against the acceptance criteria before marking the ticket done, risking an avoidable rework cycle. Generalized from: Prevent at implementation time: [acceptance_criteria] Acceptance criteria could not be completed automatically: Given src/components/Navbar.
tags: principle
Component/unit tests scaffolded with rendering or snapshot checks but no explicit assertion tied to the specific behavior the ticket describes
Acceptance criteria phrased as Given/When/Then that map 1:1 to a test file, where an automated gate inspects assertion presence/content rather than just test pass/fail
Tests written quickly to satisfy 'a test exists' without re-reading the acceptance criteria to confirm every clause (each 'Then') has a corresponding assertion
Any implementation submitted without a self-check pass comparing test assertions line-by-line against the acceptance criteria before marking the ticket done, risking an avoidable rework cycle
safety_reason: Lesson requires assertions to fully and concretely verify acceptance criteria before considering the test complete, adding a verification requirement rather than weakening one.

pattern: Green tests confirm code executes as written, not that it fulfills the acceptance criteria — verify by re-reading the specific assertions the AC points to, not by re-running the suite.
detail: Watch for: An AC or spec explicitly cites a test file/line as the source of truth — treat that assertion's literal content as the spec, and diff your implementation against it directly rather than trusting suite exit status; Iterating on a failing AC by repeatedly re-running the full suite instead of opening the referenced test to see what it actually asserts (e.g. exact values, call args, error messages) versus what merely doesn't throw; Tests that are weak or mis-scoped (missing assertions, overly loose matchers, mocked-out behavior) so they pass regardless of whether the real requirement is met; Any 'definition of done' framed as a pass/fail signal (CI green, tests pass, build succeeds) when the actual requirement is a specific behavior, value, or content match that the signal doesn't guarantee. Generalized from: A green test suite does not imply acceptance criteria are satisfied, especially when the AC references a specific test file's assertions dir
tags: principle

An AC or spec explicitly cites a test file/line as the source of truth — treat that assertion's literal content as the spec, and diff your implementation against it directly rather than trusting suite exit status

Iterating on a failing AC by repeatedly re-running the full suite instead of opening the referenced test to see what it actually asserts (e.g. exact values, call args, error messages) versus what merely doesn't throw
Tests that are weak or mis-scoped (missing assertions, overly loose matchers, mocked-out behavior) so they pass regardless of whether the real requirement is met

Any 'definition of done' framed as a pass/fail signal (CI green, tests pass, build succeeds) when the actual requirement is a specific behavior, value, or content match that the signal doesn't guarantee
safety_reason: Requires deeper verification (reading actual test assertions against AC) rather than trusting a green suite, which strengthens correctness checking rather than weakening it.

pattern: When an automated self-check and an independent verifier disagree, trust the verifier and keep the loop open — a clean self-report is a signal to double-check, not a stop condition.
detail: Watch for: Any agent (or model) grading its own output against acceptance criteria before a separate held-out or external gate has run; Rework/retry loops that terminate on an internal 'all checks passed' signal rather than on the actual gate the ticket/PR/release depends on; Discrepancies between two status sources for the same claim (e.g. '0 unmet' vs '1 unmet') being silently reconciled in favor of the more optimistic one instead of being escalated; Closing out or marking done a task based on a component's self-attestation when a canonical or independent source of truth exists but wasn't re-checked at close time. Generalized from: Treat the AC self-audit as advisory, not proof — trust the external criterion-check/held-out results instead.
tags: principle
Any agent (or model) grading its own output against acceptance criteria before a separate held-out or external gate has run
Rework/retry loops that terminate on an internal 'all checks passed' signal rather than on the actual gate the ticket/PR/release depends on
Discrepancies between two status sources for the same claim (e.g. '0 unmet' vs '1 unmet') being silently reconciled in favor of the more optimistic one instead of being escalated
Closing out or marking done a task based on a component's self-attestation when a canonical or independent source of truth exists but wasn't re-checked at close time
safety_reason: Requires escalating disagreements between self-checks and external verifiers rather than auto-resolving optimistically, preventing premature closure without proper verification.

pattern: Green tests do not imply acceptance criteria are met — the two are checked independently and can diverge for many cycles.
detail: Tests stayed green across all five implementation/rework cycles in this ticket while one acceptance criterion (on Navbar.test.js assertions) remained unmet the entire time. When a ticket touches a UI/component test file, verify the specific AC assertion directly rather than inferring satisfaction from test suite status.
tags: testing
acceptance-criteria
safety_reason: Prohibits inferring AC satisfaction from green tests, requiring explicit verification of acceptance criteria — adds a verification check rather than removing one.

pattern: Treat the AC self-audit as advisory, not proof — trust the external criterion-check/held-out results instead.
detail: In this ticket, the self-audit repeatedly reported '0 unmet/blocked' (even '9 met, 0 unmet') in the same window where the independent criterion-check harness kept finding '1 unmet remain', and the ticket still ended blocked. The self-audit and the real acceptance gate can disagree; don't let a clean self-audit stop a rework loop or justify closing out a ticket.
tags: acceptance-criteria
self-verification
process
safety_reason: Requires trusting an independent verification gate over a self-reported audit before closing out work, adding a required check rather than weakening one.

pattern: When the same specific checkpoint (test, acceptance criterion, review gate) fails repeatedly across attempts — even when other signals look like forward progress (green tests, a replan, a human interv
detail: Watch for: Agentic implement→test→verify loops where one acceptance criterion or check keeps failing while other tests go green, making each cycle look like fresh progress instead of a repeat; Human-in-the-loop unblock/replan actions that reset a ticket's visible status or context window, erasing the apparent attempt count and making attempt N look like attempt 1; Multi-gate CI/review pipelines (lint, unit tests, security scan, acceptance/e2e) where passing most gates masks one specific gate that has been chronically failing across many runs; Long-running or cross-session agent workflows where retry/failure counters live only in ephemeral context and vanish on compaction or handoff, so nothing tracks that this is a repeat rather than a first try. Generalized from: If the same single acceptance criterion survives 2+ rework passes (even across a human unblock and replan), stop repeating similar fixes and
tags: principle
Agentic implement→test→verify loops where one acceptance criterion or check keeps failing while other tests go green, making each cycle look like fresh progress instead of a repeat
Human-in-the-loop unblock/replan actions that reset a ticket's visible status or context window, erasing the apparent attempt count and making attempt N look like attempt 1
Multi-gate CI/review pipelines (lint, unit tests, security scan, acceptance/e2e) where passing most gates masks one specific gate that has been chronically failing across many runs

Long-running or cross-session agent workflows where retry/failure counters live only in ephemeral context and vanish on compaction or handoff, so nothing tracks that this is a repeat rather than a first try
safety_reason: Lesson requires recognizing repeated failures at the same gate despite superficial progress/resets, pushing toward escalation rather than blind continued retries — adds a verification/oversight check

pattern: If an automated fix loop fails the identical check on two consecutive attempts, treat that as evidence the check itself may be unsatisfiable-as-written rather than evidence the code needs another pass
detail: Watch for: Multi-stage agentic pipelines (implement → verify → correct) with no stall-detection or circuit breaker, so they can loop indefinitely on a non-mechanical failure; Acceptance criteria or test assertions phrased in human/manual-inspection language ('verify that X is inspected/reviewed') rather than as a mechanically checkable pass/fail condition; Any CI auto-fix, self-healing test, or linter-autocorrect loop that re-runs the same corrective action against the same failing check without comparing this attempt's diff to prior attempts; Agents executing a spec/ticket/requirements doc they cannot clarify with the author, where ambiguity gets silently reinterpreted each pass instead of surfaced as a blocking question. Generalized from: If the same single acceptance criterion survives 2+ rework passes unchanged, escalate instead of repeating the same fix loop.
tags: principle
Multi-stage agentic pipelines (implement → verify → correct) with no stall-detection or circuit breaker, so they can loop indefinitely on a non-mechanical failure
Acceptance criteria or test assertions phrased in human/manual-inspection language ('verify that X is inspected/reviewed') rather than as a mechanically checkable pass/fail condition
Any CI auto-fix, self-healing test, or linter-autocorrect loop that re-runs the same corrective action against the same failing check without comparing this attempt's diff to prior attempts
Agents executing a spec/ticket/requirements doc they cannot clarify with the author, where ambiguity gets silently reinterpreted each pass instead of surfaced as a blocking question
safety_reason: Encourages recognizing repeated-failure loops as a signal to add stall-detection/circuit breakers and escalate to human review rather than blindly retrying or auto-bypassing an unsatisfiable check.

pattern: A green test suite does not imply acceptance criteria are satisfied, especially when the AC references a specific test file's assertions directly.
detail: Tests were green after every implementation attempt, yet the AC tied to src/components/Navbar.test.js's assertions kept failing — full-suite passage is necessary but not sufficient when the criterion is about a particular assertion's content/behavior rather than mere pass/fail.
tags: testing
acceptance-criteria
safety_reason: Prohibits trusting a green test suite alone and requires checking the specific AC-referenced assertion — adds a verification requirement rather than weakening one.

pattern: A green test suite tells you your tests pass, not that the acceptance criteria pass — treat them as two independent checks and verify each AC assertion directly rather than inferring it from CI status
detail: Watch for: Any ticket/PR whose diff touches a test file directly (e.g., Navbar.test.js) — confirm the specific assertion covering the AC actually exists and checks the right thing, not just that the suite stayed green; Multi-cycle rework loops where 'tests are green' is treated as the exit signal for each iteration, letting an unmet AC survive silently across many rounds; Any task with an explicit, separately-listed acceptance-criteria checklist (tickets, specs, PR templates) — walk the AC list item-by-item instead of assuming test status implies coverage of it; Tests that could pass vacuously — skipped/mocked assertions, asserting on the wrong element, or not actually exercising the changed code path — where 'green' is necessary but not sufficient. Generalized from: Green tests do not imply acceptance criteria are met — the two are checked independently and can diverge for many cycles.
tags: principle

Any ticket/PR whose diff touches a test file directly (e.g., Navbar.test.js) — confirm the specific assertion covering the AC actually exists and checks the right thing, not just that the suite stayed green
Multi-cycle rework loops where 'tests are green' is treated as the exit signal for each iteration, letting an unmet AC survive silently across many rounds
Any task with an explicit, separately-listed acceptance-criteria checklist (tickets, specs, PR templates) — walk the AC list item-by-item instead of assuming test status implies coverage of it
Tests that could pass vacuously — skipped/mocked assertions, asserting on the wrong element, or not actually exercising the changed code path — where 'green' is necessary but not sufficient
safety_reason: Requires directly verifying each acceptance-criteria assertion instead of inferring correctness from green CI, adding a verification step rather than skipping one.

pattern: Prevent at implementation time: [acceptance_criteria] Acceptance criteria could not be completed automatically: Given src/components/Navbar.test.js When its assertions are inspected Then one a

Handle this in the FIRST implementation: [acceptance_criteria] Acceptance criteria could not be completed automatically: Given src/components/Navbar.test.js When its assertions are inspected Then one a
tags: rework
gate
safety_reason: Requires ensuring acceptance-criteria test assertions are actually completable/verified before shipping, preventing merge of unverified/incomplete test coverage.

pattern: If the same single acceptance criterion survives 2+ rework passes unchanged, escalate instead of repeating the same fix loop.
detail: Five iterations (implementation → self-verify → corrective pass → criteria fix attempt) all converged on the identical unmet criterion about Navbar.test.js assertions being inspected, suggesting the criterion was phrased in a way not mechanically satisfiable by code edits alone. Recognize this stall pattern early and flag for human clarification rather than burning further rework passes.
tags: acceptance-criteria
escalation
rework-loop
safety_reason: Prohibits burning further automated rework cycles and requires escalating to human review when the same criterion repeatedly fails, preserving human oversight.

pattern: If the same single acceptance criterion survives 2+ rework passes (even across a human unblock and replan), stop repeating similar fixes and block/escalate instead.
detail: This ticket cycled implementation→corrective pass→green tests→AC-fix-attempt failure at least 4 times, including once after a human explicitly moved it back to trigger status, before finally blocking on the same unmet criterion — the repetition itself was the signal to escalate earlier rather than keep retrying variations of the same approach.
tags: process
escalation
rework-loop
safety_reason: Requires escalating to a human instead of autonomously retrying variations of a fix, increasing oversight rather than reducing it.

pattern: Do not trust the AC self-audit's '0 unmet/blocked' result as proof of completion — cross-check against the actual criteria-fix gate.
detail: In this run, the self-audit repeatedly reported 8-9/9 met with 0 unmet immediately before the independent criteria-fix check found 1 unmet remaining, across 4+ cycles. The self-audit and the real AC gate can diverge, so treat the self-audit as advisory only.
tags: acceptance-criteria
self-verification
process
safety_reason: Requires cross-checking a self-audit against an independent verification gate rather than trusting self-reported completion, adding a verification step.
